Devdutt Padikkal's quickfire 61 also proved crucial in the successful chase, while Ishan Kishan's 80 wasn't enough for SRH.

His journey from free online lessons to a billion-dollar valuation highlights a unique path to success in the competitive Indian education market.

He anchored RCB's chase with an unbeaten 69, guiding them to a six-wicket victory over Sunrisers Hyderabad.  Devdutt Padikkal's explosive 61 set the stage for the dominant win, while Ishan Kishan's 80 was the highlight for SRH.

title: ‘Dhurandhar: The Revenge’ makes over Rs 1,200 crore worldwide in 10 days
description: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' is performing strongly at the box office, crossing Rs 1,200 crore worldwide after a 10-day run. The action-thriller saw a significant surge on its second Saturday, contributing to its impressive domestic and global collections. The film continues the story of an undercover agent battling terrorism.

title: Adani Defence delivers first batch of 2,000 LMGs to Army ahead of schedule
description: The delivery of the first batch of the 7.62 mm-calibre weapon, manufactured at the company’s small arms complex on the outskirts of Gwalior, was completed in seven months well ahead of the contracted schedule. The company spokesperson said the total order for the LMGs stands at around 40,000. The LMG weighs 8 kg and is 1,100 mm long and it has an effective range of 1,000 m, according to the company’s data. Prahar has a rate of fire of 600–750 rounds per minute.

title: Bengaluru professor who called student 'terrorist' 13 times suspended
description: In a shocking turn of events, a professor from PES University has been suspended following the release of a video where he repeatedly referred to a minority student as a 'terrorist' during a lecture. The incident, which took place on March 24, has prompted the police to initiate a suo motu criminal case.

title: Himachal cops question Bangladeshi student for ‘visiting’ temples as ‘eunuch’

description: Pawan Kalyan's 'Ustaad Bhagat Singh' has garnered over Rs 90 crore worldwide, but its theatrical run shows a noticeable slowdown after a strong opening week. While the film relies heavily on Kalyan's star power, mixed reviews highlight underwritten characters and a routine narrative, despite technical competence and energetic performances.

title: Man decapitates ice vendor, puts severed head on stove in Barabanki
description: In a disturbing turn of events in Barabanki, a middle-aged man allegedly decapitated an ice vendor following a heated dispute. After the brutal act, the perpetrator tried to incinerate the head inside his dwelling. Quick-thinking locals intervened, detaining him until the authorities arrived. A formal complaint has been filed against the man.

description: The maximum and minimum temperatures are likely to be around 38°C and 25°C respectively,” according to IMD bulletin

title: Why did Puka Nacua’s accuser suddenly back off? Legal twist fuels controversy
description: Puka Nacua received a legal boost after his accuser withdrew a temporary restraining order, though the civil lawsuit remains active. The Los Angeles Rams star denies all allegations. His attorney called the claims “meritless” and hinted at a defamation lawsuit, as the case continues to unfold alongside Nacua’s looming contract extension discussions.

title: ‘No Kings, No ICE, No war’: Protests draw crowds across US and Europe against Trump policies, what you need to know

description: The Indianapolis Colts are exploring options to fix their struggling pass rush after missing out on Trey Hendrickson. Trade buzz around Myles Garrett has linked him to Indianapolis, especially with a potential rule change easing future draft pick trades. With cap space and urgency, the Colts could pursue a bold move to land an elite defensive playmaker.

title: North Korea's Kim inspects solid-fuel engine, new tank as Pyongyang steps up military development
description: KCNA said the new high-thrust solid-fuel engine had a maximum thrust of 2,500 ‌kilonewtons and that the ground test formed part of a new five-year defence development plan ‌aimed at upgrading the country’s strategic strike capabilities

title: Iran reports strike at Bushehr nuclear plant as focus shifts to a new strait
description: An Iranian strike injured 12 US troops in Saudi Arabia, two of them seriously, in an attack on Prince Sultan airbase on Friday, two people briefed on the matter said. The combined missile and drone attack amounted to one of the most serious breaches of American air defences in the war. At least two KC-135 aerial refuelling planes also suffered significant damage.

title: Fact check: Did Cardi B really owe Stefon Diggs $1M?
description: Rumors linking Cardi B and Stefon Diggs went viral after a social media post claimed a fallout and a $1 million dispute. The speculation grew when an alleged deleted Instagram story surfaced. However, there is no verified proof supporting these claims. Neither party has responded publicly, and no credible reports confirm the narrative, making the controversy largely driven by unverified online speculation.

title: Sri Lanka’s supplies hit, India sends fuel shipment
Fact check: Bradley Dack, Olivia Attwood cheating claims and marriage truth?
description: Bradley Dack is facing multiple cheating claims as several women speak out, according to The Sun. At the same time, Daily Mail reports reveal he and Olivia Attwood were never legally married despite their 2023 ceremony. The couple had planned to register later, but Olivia backed out after discovering issues, leading to their split earlier this year.

title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce NDA speculation takes dramatic twist
description: Rumors about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce enforcing strict NDAs at their wedding spread quickly after Graham Norton joked about signing them. Fans and media took it seriously, fueling speculation about a secretive ceremony. Norton later clarified it was a joke that got misreported. There is no confirmed NDA, and the couple has not shared any official wedding details yet.

title: Musk in call with Trump, Modi: NYT; Govt says only the two leaders spoke
80% Indians cleared by tests are struck by heart attack
description: At the core of the problem is a mismatch. Most of these risk calculators were designed using Western populations, where heart disease tends to occur later. In India, it strikes earlier and behaves differently — the study found the average age of patients was just 54, underscoring how premature heart disease has become.

title: West Asia crisis: Rajnath Singh calls for swift decisions, long-term readiness
description: Mr. Singh made the remarks while chairing the first meeting of the Informal Group of Ministers (IGoM) set up to monitor the situation in West Asia

title: Relief for drivers: No jail for 1st-time pollution, road safety norms violations
description: In the case of noise pollution-related violations, the bill proposes a warning for the first offence, while repeat offence will attract a penalty of up to Rs 10,000. Similarly, failure to comply with an order or contravening provisions of the Electricity Act or rules, which can currently land a person in jail for three months along with a fine of Rs 1 lakh, is proposed to be converted into a fine of Rs 10,000 to Rs 10 Lakh.

title: Parliament to be adjourned on April 2, to meet after assembly polls
description: A fixed date for Parliament’s meeting to discuss what are likely to be two bills, one for delimitation based on 2011 census and another for amending constitutional provisions for the rollout of women’s quota in one-third of Lok Sabha and assembly seats, will convey the govt’s determination in no uncertain terms.

title: It’s a waiting game at the moment: Watson on Pathirana’s availability
Vulnerable need quick relief amid West Asia conflict: Finance ministry report
description: The report said the impact of the conflict on India will be felt through four channels: (a) supply disruptions to oil, gas and fertilisers and more importantly, to exports as well, (b) higher import prices, (c) higher logistics costs (e.g., freight and insurance) and (d) a possible decline in remittances by Indians in the Gulf countries. (The slowdown in exports to Gulf countries may not be particularly consequential for overall exports.)

title: India stands alone against China-backed WTO investment pact
description: While India is keen to find a solution, for almost 30 years, it has used this as a bargaining chip at every meeting, realising that an imposition of duty on streaming and downloads is set to generate revenue for it and others and put those such as the US at a disadvantage. But, for a change, India, the US, Russia and Pakistan are on the same side in their pitch for a reform of the 31-year-old multilateral body.

title: West Asia crisis affecting LPG cylinder supply to certain parts of store network: Jubilant FoodWorks
description: “We wish to inform that due to the ongoing geopolitical situation in West Asia, there are supply constraints on the distribution of commercial LPG across the country,” Jubilant FoodWorks Ltd (JFL) said in a regulatory filing

title: FSSAI makes food business permits permanent
description: Under new rules notified by Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI), food licences and registrations will now remain valid permanently, unless cancelled for violations. This means lakhs of restaurants, small eateries and vendors will no longer need to renew their licences every few years. The move is part of a wider overhaul aimed at making compliance easier while tightening actual food safety checks.

title: Government’s failure responsible for supply crunch, panic among people, says Pramod Tiwari
description: Mr. Tiwari further alleged that the BJP Government repeatedly makes such statements, which create an atmosphere of panic in the country

title: OBC and EBC voters are not happy with Nitish Kumar moving to Rajya Sabha, says Anand Mohan
description: Expressing his apprehension, Mr. Mohan added that in days to come or in near future, the ruling parties will not get any benefit from the decision of Nitish Kumar leaving Bihar politics and moving to Rajya Sabha

title: India denies Musk joined Modi-Trump call over war, refutes US media report
description: Quoting two anonymous US officials, the report said it was unclear why Musk was on the call or whether he spoke. “His companies have taken on significant investment from sovereign wealth funds in countries in the Middle East, including Saudi Arabia and Qatar. Musk has also long coveted a greater commercial presence in India,” it said.

title: Bombay Court urges mediation in Kalyani family feud
description: The siblings Baba Kalyani, Sugandha Hiremath, and Gaurishankar Kalyani, and their children have been fighting legal battles on multiple fronts for several years now. At stake is ancestral wealth estimated to be over ₹1 lakh crore

title: Vijaypat Singhania, ‘The Complete Man’, passes away
description: Vijaypat Singhania's family had acquired the company from ED Sassoon & Co in 1944, when it was primarily a manufacturer of woollen blankets. Under his leadership, Raymond evolved into a diversified conglomerate, expanding into synthetic fabrics, denim, steel, industrial files and cement, while also becoming one of India’s most recognised clothing brands.

title: Glenn Phillips is a brilliant player: GT wicketkeeper-batter Jos Buttler
description: "I'm not a selector or the captain, so that's a conversation for them. Glenn Phillips is a brilliant player; He is a really dynamic player, obviously a fantastic batter, can bowl off-spin, one of the best fielders in the world," says Buttler

title: PM Modi opens Jewar airport, flags ethanol push as shield against energy crisis
Shane Watson excited to work with Cameron Green as KKR prepares for IPL 2026

title: Expedite PNG clearances: Govt to municipal bodies, other agencies
description: Setting a target to add 50 lakh new piped gas connections to the existing base of 1.6 crore, at a round table here, the minister called for accelerating expansion on a mission mode, and underlined the need for single-window approvals, integration with urban planning, last-mile connectivity and convergence with ongoing urban initiatives.

title: PM, Saudi crown prince agree on need to keep shipping lines open
description: Underscoring India’s energy and diaspora interests in the Gulf, Modi has twice spoken to leaders of all GCC countries since the conflict started and condemned the violation of their sovereignty and territorial integrity. India, however, is yet to condemn the US-Israel strikes on Iran. This was the second telephonic conversation between the PM and the crown prince since the West Asia conflict started on Feb 28.

title: IPL 2026: RCB pays tribute to 2025 stampede victims; 11 reserved seats at M. Chinnaswamy
description: Notably, these seats are set to remain permanently unoccupied during all IPL and international matches. Ahead of the IPL 2026 opener, players from both RCB and SRH also observed a one-minute silence to pay tribute to the fans who lost their lives in 2025

title: Delhi shocker: Gang lures man using dating app, robs him of Rs 7 lakh
description: A man was lured to a meeting via a dating app in Nehru Place, only to be abducted, assaulted, and robbed of ₹7 lakh. Five individuals, including a woman, have been apprehended by Delhi Police following a thorough investigation involving CCTV analysis and digital tracking. The victim was forced to make UPI transfers before being abandoned.

title: IAF starts work on ‘Vayu Baan’, India’s first helicopter-dropped drone project
description: Vayu Baan is an indigenous air-launched effects (ALE) programme designed to integrate unmanned aerial systems directly with manned rotary-wing platforms. It is a small, autonomous drone designed to be dropped from a moving helicopter mid-flight. Once released, the drone unfolds its wings, activates its propulsion and operates as either a surveillance asset or a precision-guided munition (kamikaze drone).

title: 15 lakh Bengal voters in limbo due to missing e-signatures
description: EC officials said 60 lakh voters were initially marked for scrutiny after a special intensive revision (SIR) of rolls, with over 700 judicial officers assigned to review them. By March 23 evening, around 29 lakh cases had been cleared. Bengal CEO Manoj Agarwal said names carrying e-signatures till 5pm would be published in the first supplementary list. The list released around midnight carried about 10 lakh names.

title: PM Modi backs ethanol blending as key to cutting crude imports
description: “Farmers’ efforts in this have given a lot of relief to the country at this time of global crisis,” he said. Ethanol production, he added, has not only benefited the nation but also farmers, with approximately Rs 1.5 lakh crore worth of foreign exchange being saved. Jewar is a gateway to the sugarcane belts of western UP. Sugarcane feedstock is a key source of ethanol.

title: Former sarpanch dies waiting 4 hours for LPG in 41°C heat in Maharashtra
description: A former sarpanch died while waiting for an LPG cylinder in Akola's sweltering heat. Shankarrao Sirsat collapsed after hours in the queue. The incident has sparked anger and questions about the administration's preparedness for essential supply. Citizens are demanding regular LPG availability. Political leaders have criticized the government's arrangements.

In Jammu, Kathua district has the highest number of such schools at 508. In Kashmir, Baramulla district tops the list of such schools with 396.

title: After Zoji La avalanche deaths, Ladakh hill council stakes claim on Zero Point
description: Addressing a press conference in Drass, LAHDC-Kargil councillors praised the Army, civil administration and Ladakh Police for prompt rescue efforts and retrieval of bodies from the snow following the accident on Friday afternoon. However, they expressed surprise over “J&K Police investigating the incident on Saturday morning”. While J&K claimed the area to be part of its territory, its administrative machinery was always slow to respond when any mishap occurred there, they alleged.

description: Virat Kohli's dropped catch in the IPL opener against Sunrisers Hyderabad was a moment of drama, but he later redeemed himself by dismissing the same batter. Ishan Kishan's explosive 80 powered SRH to 201/9, despite a strong bowling performance from RCB's Jacob Duffy. The match saw several crucial wickets fall, with Kishan's innings being a highlight.

Massive blaze at Kuwait airport after Iran drone strike, fuel tanks hit
description: A massive blaze at Kuwait International Airport, triggered by a suspected Iranian drone strike on fuel storage facilities, has been extinguished after nearly 58 hours. The attack caused significant damage to radar systems, with Kuwait blaming Iran and its proxies. This incident escalates regional tensions as Iran expands its aerial campaign.

8th rape case against Ashok Kharat: Two more FIRs against bogus astrologer
description: Nashik police have registered two new cases against astrologer Ashok Kharat, bringing the total to ten. Allegations include repeated sexual assaults on a woman and cheating a trader out of Rs 2.6 lakh. The woman claims Kharat exploited her marital issues, while the trader was defrauded with fake gemstones.

Phil Salt steals the show with outrageous one-handed catch to remove Ishan Kishan - Watch
description: Phil Salt's spectacular one-handed catch ended Ishan Kishan's blistering 80-run knock, a pivotal moment in the IPL 2026 opener.  Despite Kishan's brilliance, which anchored Sunrisers Hyderabad's innings, Salt's fielding heroics, including another debated boundary catch, shifted momentum. Hyderabad ultimately posted 201/9, a total that felt short after Salt's impactful contributions.

Bangladesh denies lifting IPL broadcast ban, says suspension still in place
description: In a statement issued on Saturday and reported by Bangladesh news outlet Daily Star, the Bangladesh broadcasting ministry said the previous government’s order is still effective. It added that it has sought the opinion of the ministry of youth and sports, with a letter sent on March 25, 2026, and any further decision will depend on that response.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang on how he manages 60 direct reportees who are mostly engineers
description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang leads over sixty direct reports. He avoids one-on-one meetings, favoring collective problem-solving sessions. This approach ensures all team members receive the same information and can contribute. Huang believes in 'extreme co-design' and expects active participation. He is willing to address employees who do not contribute as expected.

title: CEOs of two of biggest American companies, Coca Cola & Walmart, are leaving due to AI
description: Two prominent CEOs, from Coca-Cola and Walmart, have cited the rise of AI as a factor in their decisions to step down. Both leaders indicated that the evolving business landscape, driven by AI, necessitates new leadership for future growth phases. Their departures highlight a significant technological shift impacting executive decision-making at major corporations.

title: Meta is rebranding employee titles, roles as it goes AI-native: What it said in memo
description: Meta's Reality Labs is undergoing a significant internal overhaul, rebranding employees as 'AI builders' and forming smaller, AI-focused teams. This strategic shift aims to boost engineering productivity and product quality, with CEO Mark Zuckerberg envisioning AI dramatically transforming work.  These new 'AI pods' will foster cross-functional collaboration, with dedicated leads managing operations and performance.
